mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate compresses the equity discount rate and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Effective federal funds rate at 0.08% as of Oct 2021
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A three-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals weakening labor demand and triggers negative S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations are cut.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in consumer prices and deceleration in industrial production create a stagflationary discount-rate and earnings double-hit, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term does not significantly improve R-squared over main effects alone (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales signal robust consumer demand that translates into corporate revenue beats, generating positive S&P 500 returns; this mechanism is active specifically in early-cycle expansions following demand troughs.
regime_hint: early-cycle expansion with rebounding consumer spending
failure_condition: Positive 20-day return hit rate is below 55% during identified early-cycle months, or the signal underperforms the unconditional baseline in that regime.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals near-term revenue momentum for manufacturing and energy sectors, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day return frequency is less than 52% during months when INDPRO accelerates, or the Sharpe ratio of the signal is negative over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained housing starts below 1,300 thousand units for two consecutive months indicate a contraction in construction activity and downstream durable demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating payroll employment and a low federal funds rate captures a Goldilocks environment where growth is strong and financing costs are low, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and FEDFUNDS does not produce a t-statistic greater than 2.0 in predicting 20-day returns, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5% signals tight monetary conditions that sharply raise discount rates and debt-service costs, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean forward return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).
